title: Infant formula during unsafe water or power loss
summary: A conservative infant-feeding continuity card that prioritizes ready-to-feed formula when appropriate, safe water, exact preparation, clean equipment, and early professional help.
content:
A conservative infant-feeding continuity card that prioritizes ready-to-feed formula when appropriate, safe water, exact preparation, clean equipment, and early professional help.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
materials: verified safe water or ready-to-feed formula
cooler and thermometer when refrigeration is required
steps: Continue breastfeeding when possible and seek lactation or medical support early if feeding is disrupted.
When formula is needed and conditions are unsafe, prefer unopened ready-to-feed formula when available and suitable for the infant.
For powdered or concentrated formula, use verified safe water and follow the product label exactly; never dilute, concentrate, or substitute ingredients.
Prepare one feed at a time with clean hands and equipment, discard leftovers from a used bottle, and watch urine output, alertness, vomiting, and feeding ability.
warnings: Never make homemade infant formula or give plain water in place of feeds.
A very young, premature, ill, dehydrated, or poorly feeding infant needs prompt professional assessment.
common_mistakes: Starting Infant formula during unsafe water or power loss without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Infant formula during unsafe water or power loss.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Infant formula during unsafe water or power loss when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Infant formula during unsafe water or power loss when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Infant formula during unsafe water or power loss.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Infant formula during unsafe water or power loss safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Infant formula during unsafe water or power loss when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Infant formula during unsafe water or power loss.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Infant formula during unsafe water or power loss should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Infant formula during unsafe water or power loss whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Infant formula during unsafe water or power loss in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Infant formula during unsafe water or power loss after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Infant formula during unsafe water or power loss cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Infant formula during unsafe water or power loss are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Infant formula during unsafe water or power loss to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Infant formula during unsafe water or power loss.']
